revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shimoikeyama_Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

下池山古墳（しもいけやまこふん）は[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町に所在する墳丘全長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]に属す[[古墳時代]]前期前半の古墳。奈良県指定史跡。
== 概要 == [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の造営年代は[[4世紀]]前半と推定される。
== 関連項目 == *[[前方後円墳]] *[[大和古墳群]]
== 参考文献 == *「ヤマト王権の成立」『大和の考古学』奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館、1997年。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kanko/kohun_guide/kohun/index.html 天理市古墳ガイド]（奈良県天理市）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもいけやまこふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:天理市]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

[[ファイル:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|280px|下池山古墳（{{国土航空写真}}）]] '''下池山古墳'''（しもいけやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町に所在する墳丘全長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]に属す[[古墳時代]]前期前半の古墳。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）。
== 概要 == [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の造営年代は[[4世紀]]前半と推定される。
[[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[中山大塚古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}</ref>。

[[ファイル:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|280px|下池山古墳（{{国土航空写真}}）]] '''下池山古墳'''（しもいけやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町に所在する墳丘全長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。[[大和古墳群]]に属する[[古墳時代]]前期前半の[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|割竹形木棺}}{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}}]] [[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の造営年代は[[4世紀]]前半と推定される。立地や築造時期などから、被葬者は[[波多子塚古墳]]に続く系譜の者と見られ、具体的には[[倭国造]]の[[五十野宿禰]]が想定される<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm 沼津の高尾山古墳の保存問題]」『古樹紀之房間』、2015年。</ref>。
[[2014年]]（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[中山大塚古墳]]とともに「大和古墳群」として国の[[史跡]]に指定された<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *「ヤマト王権の成立」『大和の考古学』[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]、1997年。
== 関連項目 == *[[大和古墳群]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kanko/kohun_guide/kohun/index.html 天理市古墳ガイド]（奈良県天理市）
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいけやまこふん}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大和古墳群]]

revision.comment: 非考古学者による説の除去：被葬者の考察は慎重に行われるべきものであり、系図学の俗説が流布されるべきではない。

{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}} |所在地=[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長120m |築造時期=[[4世紀]]前半 |出土品= |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「下池山古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財=出土品（奈良県指定有形文化財） |埋葬施設= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=下池山古墳|mark=前方後方墳-南.png|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''下池山古墳'''（しもいけやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）、出土品は奈良県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]である。[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の築造時期は[[4世紀]]前半頃と推定される。 ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|割竹形木棺
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}} ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|割竹形木棺（別角度から） == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 下池山古墳（史跡「[[大和古墳群]]」のうち） - 2014年（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[中山大塚古墳]]・下池山古墳の3基を合わせて「大和古墳群」として指定。 === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 下池山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管。2006年（平成18年）3月31日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 下池山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *「ヤマト王権の成立」『大和の考古学』[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]、1997年。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいけやまこふん}} [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

下池山古墳の空中写真（1979年）
{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}} |所在地=[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長120m |築造時期=[[4世紀]]前半 |出土品= |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「下池山古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財=出土品（奈良県指定有形文化財） |埋葬施設= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=下池山古墳|mark=前方後方墳-南.png|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''下池山古墳'''（しもいけやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）、出土品は奈良県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]である。[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の築造時期は[[4世紀]]前半頃と推定される。 ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|割竹形木棺（奈良県指定文化財）
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}} ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|割竹形木棺（別角度から） ファイル:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品（奈良県指定文化財）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 下池山古墳（史跡「[[大和古墳群]]」のうち） - 2014年（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[中山大塚古墳]]・下池山古墳の3基を合わせて「大和古墳群」として指定。 === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 下池山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管。2006年（平成18年）3月31日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 下池山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *「ヤマト王権の成立」『大和の考古学』[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]、1997年。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいけやまこふん}} [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
下池山古墳
[[Category:下池山古墳]][[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |Image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |Location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |Image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[category:new pages]][[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun |Name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |Image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |Location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |Coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:下池山古墳]]

Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Shimo-Ikeyama Kofun]] to [[Shimoikeyama Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:下池山古墳]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|ltt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}}
