{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.comment: updating the links to the [[Ōyamato Kofun Group]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}}
