{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)

artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:竪穴式石室]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun |Group = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |Artifacts = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |Scale = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |HistoricSite = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleCulturalProperty = Artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |BurialFacility = |Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated artifacts are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995年}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron artifacts. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying artifacts, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively designated as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Excavated Artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}}
