{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year }} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. {{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |Construction = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|内行花文鏡|" to "|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

revision.comment: adding secondary category for the ones still with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=draft|12=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|12=draft|12=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]]

Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
