revision.comment: 20 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下池山古墳 up to rev 101653252
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=下池山古墳 |別名= |所属=[[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群）『前方後円墳集成』、1992年。 |画像=[[ファイル:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
下池山古墳の空中写真（1979年）
{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}} |所在地=[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長120m |築造時期=[[3世紀]]後半天理市観光協会、[https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ 下池山古墳|天理観光ガイド・天理市観光協会] |出土品= |史跡=国の[[史跡]]「下池山古墳」
（「大和古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財=出土品（奈良県指定有形文化財） |埋葬施設= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|label=下池山古墳|mark=前方後方墳-南.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''下池山古墳'''（しもいけやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]成願寺町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[大和古墳群]]（萱生支群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「大和古墳群」のうち）、出土品は奈良県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘長約120メートルの[[前方後方墳]]である。[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）から[[1996年]]（平成8年）にかけて[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]により[[竪穴式石室]]の[[発掘調査]]が行われた。多数の板石の用いられた石室は、長さが6.8メートルで、その内部からは[[木棺]]とともに鉄製品等が出土している。石室の北西側からは小石室が検出され、その内部で大形の[[内行花文鏡]]が発見された。共伴する[[遺物]]等より、古墳の築造時期は[[4世紀]]前半頃と推定される。 ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|割竹形木棺（奈良県指定文化財）
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館展示。}} ファイル:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|割竹形木棺（別角度から） ファイル:下池山古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品（奈良県指定文化財）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 下池山古墳（史跡「[[大和古墳群]]」のうち） - 2014年（平成26年）10月6日、ノムギ古墳・[[中山大塚古墳]]・下池山古墳の3基を合わせて「大和古墳群」として指定。 === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 下池山古墳出土品（考古資料） - 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館保管。2006年（平成18年）3月31日指定。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 下池山古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *「ヤマト王権の成立」『大和の考古学』[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館]]、1997年。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|00003861|大和古墳群}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいけやまこふん}} [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|ja|3世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara Prefecture|ja|天理市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Yamato Kofun Group|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} (as part of the "Yamato Kofun Group"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} between {{ill|1995|ja|1995th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|1996|ja|1996年|qq=draft|qq=draft}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large {{ill|Flower mirror|lt=mirrored bronze with flower patterns|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Yamato Kofun Group") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Yamato Kofun Group." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Yamato Kofun Group}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yamato Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]]

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup)『前方後円墳集成』, 1992. |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=}}.}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|qq=}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:天理市の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]]

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=}}.}} |location = [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|qq=}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}} between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara�]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}}

{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoikeyama Kofun]] [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:大和古墳群]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}}

[[commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361070]] Wikidata name: Shimoikeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11433419}} |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}}

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}}

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361070]] Wikidata name: Shimoikeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}}

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
