{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361070]] Wikidata name: Shimoikeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

[[co:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361070]] Wikidata name: Shimoikeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]]{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara Prefecture]], Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] between [[:en:1995|1995]] and [[:en:1996|1996]] revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]].}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]]. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]] (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum]], 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]]

{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} between {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shimoikeyama Kofun]][[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Shimoikeyama Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11433419}} |image = [[File:Shimoikeyama kohun aerial.jpg|250px]]
Aerial view of Shimoikeyama Kofun (1979)
{{small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}.}} |location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} |coordinates = {{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E|map_size:10000}} |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_size = Mound length 120 m |built = Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}}{{ill|Tenri Tourism Association|ja|天理市観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/shimoikeyamakofun/ Shimoikeyama Kofun|Tenri Tourism Guide]. |burial goods = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}: "Shimoikeyama Kofun" (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") |TangibleHistoric site = burial goods (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property) |burial = |map = {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=7.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=50|lon_sec=46.8|map_dot_label=Shimoikeyama Kofun|mark=Keyhole-shaped tomb - South.svg|marksize=23|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|7.6|N|135|50|46.8|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (下池山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture}}, Narogaji Town. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Kayao subgroup). Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} between {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. Designated as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} (as part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods are registered as Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site. == Overview == The mound is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a length of approximately 120 meters. Excavations conducted by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} between {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} and {{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}} revealed a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. This chamber, measuring 6.8 meters in length and constructed using numerous stone slabs, contained a wooden coffin and iron burial goods. Additionally, a smaller chamber was discovered on the northwest side of the main chamber, where a large [[:en:Flower mirror|mirrored bronze with flower patterns]] was unearthed. Based on accompanying burial goods, the tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺-2.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} File:下池山古墳出土 割竹形木棺.JPG|Split bamboo-style wooden coffin (from another angle) File:下池山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (Nara Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property)
{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * '''Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (part of the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster") – On October 6, 2014, the {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|ja|中山大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}, and Shimoikeyama Kofun were collectively Historic site as the "Ōyamato Kofun Cluster." === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic site === * '''burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun''' (archaeological materials) – Preserved and displayed at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}. Designated on March 31, 2006. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} (Kashihara City, Unebi Town) – Houses and displays burial goods from Shimoikeyama Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * "Formation of the Yamato State," in ''Archaeology of Yamato,'' {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}, 1997. == External Links == * {{National Cultural Property Database|401|00003861|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|下池山古墳|version=101653252|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:下池山古墳]] {{Commons category|Shimoikeyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11361070}} [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Ōyamato Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]
